title: A comparison of European, Polish, Slovenian and British EQ-5D-3L value sets using a Hungarian sample of 18 chronic diseases
abstract: Our results suggest that the choice of value set largely influences the health state utility results in chronic diseases, and might have a significant impact on health policy decisions.
summary_en: The EQ-5D-3L instrument measures health-state utility for cost-effectiveness analysis. But the "value set" (which assigns utility to each state) can differ across countries. Does the choice matter for concrete policy decisions? This paper compares European, Polish, Slovenian, and British value sets on a Hungarian sample of patients with 18 chronic diseases. The conclusion: value-set choice substantially influences health-state utility results in chronic disease — and thus affects health-policy decisions. An important methodological caution for Hungarian cost-effectiveness analyses.
data_used: Hungarian sample of patients with 18 chronic diseases, evaluated against European, Polish, Slovenian, and British EQ-5D-3L value sets.
policy_relevance: Directly relevant to Hungarian health-technology assessment (GYEMSZI, NEAK) and to drug-reimbursement decisions. The paper supports the development of a Hungarian-specific value set for EQ-5D-3L to avoid systematic biases from using foreign sets. Relevant to Hungarian HTA methodology reform. Caveat: chronic-disease focus; acute and preventive-care implications may differ.
